meta_title: For Freelancers: Git with AI to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Git with AI to Solve Can't Debug Code
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with bugs. Learn how to use Git with AI, Antigravity, and Claude Code to debug faster, impress clients, and ship professional SaaS apps.
content.answer_block: Stop staring at broken code. By integrating AI-assisted debugging into your Git workflow, you can isolate errors, generate automated unit tests, and resolve logic gaps in minutes. Using tools like Antigravity and Claude Code turns your repository into a conversational workspace, transforming complex stack traces into actionable, production-ready fixes immediately.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking, the client is waiting, and you are staring at a cryptic stack trace that makes zero sense. You have rewritten the function three times, checked the documentation, and still, the application crashes on deployment. This is the 'freelancer's wall'—where time-to-market stalls and frustration peaks.

But what if your codebase could talk back? By leveraging Git-integrated AI, you stop treating your terminal like a graveyard for errors and start treating it like a high-speed diagnostic machine. We are moving beyond copy-pasting into LLMs; we are implementing a professional-grade feedback loop that fixes bugs while maintaining your repository's integrity.

steps.title: Establish the Contextual Baseline
steps.description: Before debugging, you must provide the AI with the current state of your repository. This prevents hallucinations by grounding the AI in your actual file structure and dependencies.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current directory structure and identify the primary entry point for the application. List all relevant environment variables needed for a clean build.

steps.title: Isolate the Error with Git Diffs
steps.description: Use Git to pinpoint exactly when the bug was introduced. By feeding the specific commit diff into the AI, you isolate the logic change that caused the regression.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Compare the last two commits in this repository. Identify the logic change in the Auth module that likely caused the recent 500 error.
steps.claude_code_command: git diff HEAD~1..HEAD | claude chat 'Analyze this diff for potential side effects'

steps.title: Automated Unit Test Generation
steps.description: Don't just fix the code; verify it. Use the AI to generate a failing test case that reproduces the bug, ensuring the fix is permanent and regression-proof.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Jest unit test file that specifically targets the failing function in userController.js. The test should fail based on the current bug report.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate unit test for userController.js'

steps.title: Execute Targeted Refactoring
steps.description: Apply the fix using the AI's suggestions, ensuring that the code adheres to your project's existing style guide and architectural patterns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the identified logic error in userController.js. Ensure the fix follows DRY principles and handles edge cases for undefined user objects.

steps.title: Verify and Commit
steps.description: Finalize the fix by running the test suite and committing the changes with a descriptive, AI-generated commit message.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Verify the fix against the generated test suite. Once passed, write a professional commit message following conventional commits format.
steps.claude_code_command: npm test && git commit -m 'fix: resolve race condition in user authentication'

pro_tips: Always use a dedicated 'debug' branch when working with AI to keep your main branch clean and deployable.
Feed your package.json into the AI context first so it understands your dependency versioning constraints.
Use AI to document your fixes by asking it to update the README.md with the resolved issue details immediately.
If the AI suggests a breaking change, ask it to provide a migration script instead of just the code fix.
Set your Claude Code context to ignore node_modules to speed up indexing and reduce token noise.
content.next_steps: Once your bug is resolved, implement a CI/CD pipeline that automatically triggers these AI-debugging scripts whenever a test fails in your staging environment.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting bugs dictate your development speed. Master the art of AI-assisted debugging and turn your freelance workflow into a high-velocity engine. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
